Dungeons & Dragons: Dungeon Scrawlers – Heroes of Undermountain
Edge of Darkness: Cliffs of Coldharbor
Edge of Darkness: Emissaries of the Vale
Edge of Darkness: Guilds Power Pack Expansion
Edge of Darkness: Sands of Dunestar
Eminent Domain
item.comment:
Played a few play-tests.
After playing Race for the Galaxy, Dominion, and Glory to Rome; I find that I like Eminent Domain more than Race for the Galaxy and Glory to Rome. The deckbuilding aspect is their as a mechanic and not the whole game so it is actually quite different from Dominion. Race for the Galaxy and Glory to Rome are both very dependent on what cards become available and rather you can get your strategy going based on that. Eminent Domain allows you to build your strategy and manipulate it over the course of the game. I see a great advantage to this. It's also fairly simply so teaching to new players should be easy.
I can't wait to get a copy.

item.name: Fortune and Glory: The Cliffhanger Game
Genesis: Battle of Champions – Tactical Collectible Card Game
Imperial Settlers: Roll & Write
item.comment: I like the solo adventure mode. It's a great puzzle. I have yet to try this multiplayer. I can see the potential for a decent game even though the only interaction is via the limited and varied favor tokens.

item.name: The Isle of Cats: Explore & Draw
It's a Wonderful World: Corruption & Ascension
KeyForge Adventures: Rise of the Keyraken
King's Court
item.comment: A game I still have from when I was a kid.

item.name: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game
item.comment:
After a few plays everyone I played with says they will definitely play again. The different scenarios and masterminds vary the difficulty quite a bit.
The only quirk is that shuffling the villain deck to have an even distribution is key. Other than that the integration of the rules and the theme are excellent.

item.name: Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Dark City
Legendary: A Marvel Deck Building Game – Guardians of the Galaxy
The Lord of the Rings: Duel for Middle-earth
The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring – Trick-Taking Game
Lords of Waterdeep
item.comment: The thin vainer of theme bothers me a little. The game play is solid though and makes up for it immensely. A work placement game with some direct player interaction by using intrigue cards. The theme is mostly based on the nameing of things. The intrigue cards due provide theme by it representing the lords manipulating other lords through their agents.

item.name: Love Letter: Legend of the Five Rings
Magic: The Gathering
item.comment:
The most cost effective way to play is to find 7 other friends and do a booster draft. So far its been great. Everyone has limited resources (cards) and you do what you can with what you got.
I've played some 2-headed giant, and with the booster draft it makes for some interesting play.
Since its been so long since I played, I had forgotten how powerful certain cards can be. I'll probably draft differently in the next draft.
Update 9 years later: Done. If you are not constantly spending money and time on this then you cannot play. So sick of TCG/CCG games.

item.name: Magic: The Gathering Universes Beyond – Doctor Who
Mansions of Madness: Second Edition – Horrific Journeys: Expansion
Mansions of Madness: Second Edition – Path of the Serpent: Expansion
Monty Python Fluxx
item.comment: This game is about the social experience and less about the rules. It was hated by the players of my one game. I thought it was a hoot.

item.name: Pendragon: The Fall of Roman Britain
Quarriors!
item.comment: If you win its strategy; if you lose its the dice. I love this game. The randomness allows the game to just be fun without having to be serious. It only works with a group that is willing to lose to luck. The variety provided by the different cards for each set of dice allows for no two games being the same.

item.name: Risk: The Lord of the Rings
Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – Collector's Edition Upgrade Pack
Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – Mystery Tales
Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island – The Book of Adventures
Robinson Crusoe: Collector's Edition
item.originalname: Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island

item.name: Shadowlord!
item.comment: A game I still have from when I was a kid.

item.name: Small World
item.comment: I like this as a two player lunch time strategy game. It's no where near as strategic as chess, but is short enough to fit within the 60 minute limit. The mixture of race and powers does provide for some very hilarious emergent stories as the sturdy ghouls recede providing support to the flying elves as they rise to glory.

Star Trek Deck Building Game: The Next Generation
Star Trek Deck Building Game: The Original Series
Star Wars: Outer Rim – Unfinished Business
Tempus Quest: Episode 0 – Some Re-Assembly Required
Terrace
item.comment: A game I still have from when I was a kid.
